hooks.prompt: The user is about to run a Bash command. Check if this command contains any of these destructive patterns: 'rm -rf /', 'rm -rf ~', 'rm -rf *', 'git push --force', 'git push -f', 'git reset --hard', 'DROP TABLE', 'DROP DATABASE', 'truncate', ':(){:|:&};:', 'mkfs', 'dd if=', '> /dev/sda'. If the command contains any of these patterns, respond with: {"decision": "block", "reason": "Blocked destructive command: [pattern]. Use with caution."}. Otherwise respond with: {"decision": "allow"}. Only check the tool input, do not run anything.

A cloud task just completed via remote_exec or remote_exec_batch. Remind the assistant to check the task results and offer to show details via remote_task_status if any task failed or had a non-zero exit code. Respond with: {"systemMessage": "Cloud task completed. If any tasks failed, offer to check details with remote_task_status."}

The conversation is ending. Check if there were any remote_exec or remote_exec_batch tool calls during this conversation that may have left running sandboxes. If so, remind the assistant to offer cancelling them via remote_cancel. Respond with: {"decision": "approve", "reason": "Session ending. Consider cancelling any orphaned cloud tasks with remote_cancel."}

hooks.command: bash -c 'REMOTE=$(git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$REMOTE" ]; then REPO=$(echo "$REMOTE" | sed -E "s|.*github\.com[:/]||" | sed "s/\.git$//"); echo "{\"systemMessage\": \"Current git repo: $REPO\"}"; fi'
